我自己睇左兩個月書，仲睇緊FAA handbook
有d野都唔係好明，有無人一齊討論下tech野？
1. 點解load factor會increase stall speed?
2. 點解conventional winglet more efficient during climbing?
求高手解答

另外有無人知CX無左FG之後個新test係咪姐係KA既compass test？


I think:

A
1. Higher load factor can be regarded as plane is "heavier"
2. Higher lift is needed for the plane to keep alt
3. Higher stall speed is needed

B
1. Winglet reduces wingtip vortices
2. Hence induced drag is lower
3. "Thrust - smaller drag " so efficiency is improved

差唔多 timeline 一齊交流下

我又問條問題交流下

" How CG affects stall speed? "

Morning

Blended winglet (744)
1. Higher pressure in the outside face of the winglet
2. Lower pressure in the inside face of the winglet
3. Lift is generated
4. Forward lift component can be generated
5. More effective when high aoa as the pressure diff is higher

Raked wingtip (77w)
1. Higher AR, less drag
2. They kind of re-distributed the wingload, no solid ans for this one from me

Try try this:

" in steady climb, the lift is smaller, greater or equal to the weight? "

有d野都唔係好明，有無人一齊討論下tech野？
1. 點解load factor會increase stall speed?
2. 點解conventional winglet more efficient during climbing?
求高手解答

另外有無人知CX無左FG之後個新test係咪姐係KA既compass test？


1. During a turn, part of the lift is acting as the centripetal force, which is the horizontal component, to pull the aircraft into the turn. Only the vertical is countering the weight of aircraft right now.
2. To maintain the vertical component of the lift for maintaining a level turn. You need to either increase speed or pull the columns back.
3. Assuming you have pull the aircraft to nearly stall already, AOA ~16-17. Yet you still do not have the lift that you need.
4. What can you do ? Increase airspeed
5. The airspeed you have to hold, in order to maintain level flight = stall speed

Bernoulli Principle可以理解做Total pressure = constant
而Total pressure ＝ Static + Dynamic
如果個Speed上升, base on dynamic pressure個Equation (1/2 rho v-squared), dynamic pressure就會上升
根據Bernoulli Principle, Dynamic pressure多左, static pressure就會相對降低
所以Speed上升, static pressure就會越低
個Wing既Design係令到上面既Airflow係快過下面
所以Static pressure difference就會Generate到Lift

I think:

A

B

3. "Thrust - smaller drag " so efficiency is improved

From PPruNe

I ain't very technical and I'm sure you will get some comprehensive replys on this. But here is a VERY simplistic explanation. There is a down force on the horizontal/ elevator which holds the nose up. If an aircraft weighs 1000 lbs. and the down force is 20 lbs., the aircraft thinks it weighs 1,020 lbs.when airborne. If that same a/c weighs 1,000 lbs but has a 100 lb. downforce then the aircraft thinks it weighs 1,100 lbs. The reason there would be higher down force would be if the CG was forward and it needed more down force to hold the nose up. The heavier aircraft the higher the stall speed. Aircraft with computers actually figure their CG and will fly at a speed with this calculation, such as a L1011. The stall no matter what the weight will always happen at the same angle of attack. . .Now I sincerely hope that someone who knows what they are talking about will set me straight.

Ps. A rear CG loaded a/c will get better fuel economy than a forward loaded CG. Why? Would a 1,020 or 1,100 get better fuel economy? Which weight would require a higher angle of attack? And the higher angle of attack will give more drag and less fuel economy. Might not should like much of a difference but an airline operating 1,000 a/c will save hundreds of thousands of $ loading their a/c with a rearward CG. . .Why do i have that feeling I am about to learn something?

個cg同stall speed 既關係cg move rearward, the down force require in order keep the nose up will be high, hence higher downforce, i.e. Weight. 呢個係槓桿原理
That's why if cg move aft, high stall speed due to high down force applied to maintain the nose up

個cg同stall speed 既關係cg move rearward, the down force require in order keep the nose up will be high, hence higher downforce, i.e. Weight. 呢個係槓桿原理
That's why if cg move aft, high stall speed due to high down force applied to maintain the nose up

好似係cg越前，stall at higher airspeed喎
Higher pitch down tendency if forward CG, it requires a nose up trim to maintain level flight. Therefore higher AoA and more drag. The critical AoA can be reached in a higher airspeed

Fly by wire means the any input by the pilots is first converted to electronic signal and then transmitted to corresponding parts eg flight control surfaces through electric circuit. It is an alternative to conventional mechanical linkage.
what are the advantages and disadvantages of FBW?

what are the advantages and disadvantages of FBW?

Advantage: Reduce the weight of aircraft, since most of the mechanical components connecting the control column and actuator are replaced by the computer and electric wire, thus reduce the cost.
Reduce the cost of maintaining mechanical components.
Smoother control in adverse weather for pilots.
Disadvantage: Less control feedback.

what are the advantages and disadvantages of FBW?

FBW其中一個重要嘅好處係有envelope protection,所有pilot inputs都會經電腦監察同調整過之後先會傳去Control surfaces,確保唔會超過左個flight envelope(777為例就有stall,overspeed同bank angle protection

另外用FBW嘅機其實都要Hydraulic power,FBW只係取代左control cable去連接機頭嘅Flight controls同Control surfaces嘅Hydraulic actuators

壞處就係無電就用唔到(當然個機會係好微
所以部份Flight controls就有Mechanical backup,即係保留番control cable,但backup只係用黎保持平飛,俾時間機師去restore power

http://code7700.com/aero_airbus_control_laws.htm

BACKUP SYSTEM
[Airbus FTCM, pg. 2.60.11] The purpose of the backup system is to allow control of the aircraft following a total loss of electrics, flight control computers, elevators, or ailerons and spoilers. It is designed to allow the crew to safely stabilise the flight path while attempting to recover a control law or restore a lost system(s). It is not intended that an approach and landing should be flown in this configuration.

我自己平時都係好容易緊張嘅人，但有嘢發生我反面好平淡應對。有次喺德國炒車衝出公路架車失控，個駄已經鎖實，但我都係不自覺咁將個駄狂chok等佢轉多少少，最後我停到個位同個架空電纜塔差一米唔夠，過多少少我就回唔到你。之後坐我側邊個fd平靜咗之後話見我平時癲癲傻傻on99，但都總算明白點解公司會揀我

睇過好多lift theories
但全部都離唔開pressure difference依樣嘢
至於點解有個difference就係唔同theories嘅分野

我自己理解就係
airflow係circular motion嘅一種，
circular motion就一定要有向心力令air貼住wing surface
依個向心力就係pressure gradient (P1>P2>P3)製造出嚟



有個定律就係：喺一個circular motion入面，越近centre of curvature嘅streamline，pressure就越低 (P1>P2>P3)
加上我哋都知air flows from high pressure to low pressure region
由此推斷，越近wing surface，air pressure 就越低

亦即係話，喺upper wing surface：
pressure drops from atmospheric level (Patm) to Pus (最近upper wing surface嘅 airstream嘅pressure)

喺lower wing surface
pressure drops from 最近lower wing surface嘅 airstream嘅pressure (方便解釋，assume佢叫Pls) to atmospheric level (Patm)

Pls>Patm WHILE Patm>Pus
所以Pls>Pus
依個就係係我哋所知嘅difference in pressure between upper and lower wing surface
